On this page

    There is no single best business credit card. The right choice depends on three things: whether you need to carry a balance (APR matters), whether you clear monthly (rewards matter), and whether your bank offers a card at all (access matters). Most cards on this list require an existing business current account with that bank. If you bank with Starling, Tide, or Monzo, your options narrow to three.

    16 cards reviewed
    Independently assessed
    Rates verified 20 March 2026
    Also Consider

    We reviewed every major UK business credit card available in March 2026, verified rates and fees against each provider’s pricing page, and ranked them by the decision that actually matters to you: what you need the card to do.

    The UK business credit card market splits into three lanes. Traditional bank cards (Lloyds, HSBC, NatWest, Santander, Metro Bank) offer the lowest APRs but require you to bank with them. Fintech cards (Capital on Tap, Moss, Funding Circle) offer higher limits and faster decisions but at higher rates. And Amex cards offer the best rewards and travel perks but with acceptance gaps across UK suppliers.

    This page covers all three. Each card is positioned by what it’s actually best for, not by a generic star rating. The difference between the cheapest and most expensive card on this list is over 10 percentage points of APR — and you may not be able to access either.

    Which Type of Card Do You Need?

    The Filter Most Comparison Sites Don’t Lead With

    Seven of the twelve cards on this page require an existing business current account with that bank. You cannot apply for a Lloyds card without a Lloyds BCA. You cannot apply for an HSBC card without an HSBC BCA. This isn’t a footnote — it’s the most important practical filter in the UK business credit card market.

    If you bank with one of the five big banks (Lloyds, HSBC, NatWest, Santander, Metro Bank), you have access to the lowest APR cards. If you don’t, your realistic options are:

    • Barclaycard Select Cashback — 25.5% APR, no account requirement, but the highest rate of the traditional bank cards
    • Capital on Tap — no account requirement, high limits, but the actual rate most applicants receive is far above the advertised floor
    • Moss — no account requirement, fast decision, but positioned as a spend management tool first
    • Funding Circle — fintech cashback card without a bank-switching requirement
    • American Express — no BCA requirement, but Amex acceptance is not universal across UK suppliers

    We lead with this filter because it narrows the field immediately. There’s no point comparing twelve cards if you can only access four.

    Detailed card reviews

    Every Card, Reviewed

    Cards are ordered by category: open-access cards first (no bank account needed), then bank-locked cards by APR, then charge cards and rewards specialists. Each card includes verified rates, our editorial assessment, and the specific scenario where it makes sense.

    From 13.86% floor APR · No annual fee · Up to £250,000 limit
    Best for high credit limits without switching banks

    Capital on Tap Business Credit Card

    The highest credit limits in the UK business card market, no bank account requirement, and no FX fees. But the floor rate is not what most applicants get.
    View Capital on Tap Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Floor APR From 13.86% variable
    Rep. APR (secondary sources) 34.65%–36.19% Uswitch, Merchant Savvy
    Annual Fee £0 (free card) / £299 (Pro card)
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for Limited companies needing £25k–£250k credit facilities without switching their business bank
    Watch out Average rate offered Oct–Dec 2025 was 46.05%. Sole traders are excluded entirely.
    Not ideal if You’re a sole trader, or you assume the “from 13.86%” rate is what you’ll actually receive
    Eligibility UK limited companies and LLPs only. Min £24k turnover. Companies House registration required.
    High limits and open access, but the advertised rate is a floor, not a guarantee
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against capitalontap.com, 20 March 2026
    25.5% APR · No annual fee · No bank account required
    Best card you can get without an existing bank account

    Barclaycard Select Cashback Business Credit Card

    The only major business credit card that doesn’t require an existing business current account. That open access is the reason to choose it.
    View Barclaycard Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 25.5% variable
    Cashback 1% on monthly spend over £2k Uncapped
    Annual Fee £0 No annual fee
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for Businesses on Starling, Tide, Monzo, or any bank without its own credit card product
    Watch out 25.5% is the highest representative APR among traditional bank cards on this list
    Not ideal if You already bank with Lloyds, HSBC, NatWest, or Santander and could get a lower rate
    Eligibility Sole traders, partnerships, LTDs. £10k–£6.5m turnover. No existing account required.
    Open access is genuinely rare in this market — but 25.5% is the price
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against barclaycard.co.uk, 20 March 2026
    34.9% rep. APR · 2% cashback (first 6 months), then 1% uncapped · No existing account required
    Fintech cashback without the bank-account barrier

    Funding Circle Cashback Business Credit Card

    A cashback card from a fintech lender. 2% for the first 6 months then 1% uncapped after that. No bank account requirement but limited companies only.
    View Funding Circle Cashback Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 34.9% variable Interest from 14.9% p.a.
    Annual Fee £0 No annual fee
    Cashback 2% first 6 months, then 1% uncapped 2% capped at £2,000 cashback
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for Limited companies wanting cashback without needing a specific bank account
    Watch out 34.9% representative APR is significantly higher than the traditional bank cards. Limited companies only, minimum 1 year trading and £30k turnover.
    Not ideal if You’re a sole trader (excluded) or you carry a balance regularly (APR is high)
    Eligibility UK limited companies only. Min 1 year trading, £30k+ turnover. fundingcircle.com.
    Fintech cashback without a bank switch — but limited companies only
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against fundingcircle.com, 20 March 2026
    34.3% rep. APR · Up to 1% cashback · Multiple employee cards · Spend controls built in
    Best for multi-card spend management

    Moss Business Credit Card

    Designed for teams that need multiple cards with per-card spend limits and real-time tracking. Less a credit card, more a spend management platform with credit built in.
    View Moss Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 34.3% variable Interest from 14.9% p.a.
    Cashback Up to 1%
    Repayment 30 or 60 day terms Flexible repayment cycle
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for Growing businesses issuing 5+ employee cards who need granular spend controls
    Watch out 34.3% representative APR. Pricing is via platform subscription, not just card fees. Check getmoss.com for current plans.
    Not ideal if You need a single card with the lowest possible APR, or you’re a sole trader
    Eligibility UK limited companies and LLPs. Check getmoss.com for full eligibility.
    Spend management first, credit card second — that’s the real positioning
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against getmoss.com, 20 March 2026
    23.7% APR · 1% cashback · £30 flat fee covers all cardholders
    Best fee structure for multi-cardholder teams

    Santander Business Cashback Credit Card

    The flat per-account fee structure means the more employee cards you issue, the better the deal. Most competitors charge per cardholder.
    View Santander Card Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 23.7% variable
    Purchase Rate 18.9% p.a. variable
    Annual Fee £30 per account Single fee covers all cardholders
    Existing Account Santander BCA required
    Best for Santander business customers issuing 3+ employee cards
    Watch out Restricted to Santander 1|2|3 BCA holders. Personal guarantees required.
    Not ideal if You don’t bank with Santander and won’t switch
    Eligibility Santander business current account required. Max 2 directors/partners. £500–£25k limits.
    The fee advantage is real for teams — one flat fee regardless of how many cards you issue
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against santander.co.uk, 20 March 2026
    15.95% APR · 14.9% purchase rate · £32 fee waived at £6k+ spend
    Lowest representative APR on the market

    Lloyds Bank Business Credit Card

    The lowest representative APR available in the UK business credit card market. But only accessible to existing Lloyds business customers.
    View Lloyds Card Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 15.95% variable
    Purchase Rate 14.9% p.a. variable
    Annual Fee £32 per cardholder Waived yr 1; waived at £6k+ annual spend
    Existing Account Lloyds BCA required
    Best for Existing Lloyds customers who carry a balance and can hit the fee waiver threshold
    Watch out £32 annual fee per card. Only waived at £6k+ annual spend per card.
    Not ideal if You don’t bank with Lloyds and would need to switch just for the rate
    Eligibility Sole traders, partnerships, company directors. Lloyds BCA required.
    The lowest rate available, if you’re already banking with Lloyds
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against lloydsbank.com, 20 March 2026
    24.3% APR · No FX fee on overseas purchases · £30/card fee waivable
    Best for businesses that spend overseas

    NatWest Business Credit Card

    The standout feature is zero foreign exchange fees on overseas card purchases. No other traditional bank card on this list offers that.
    View NatWest Card Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 24.3% variable
    Purchase Rate 16.9% p.a. variable
    Annual Fee £30 per cardholder Waived at £6k+ annual spend per card
    Existing Account NatWest BCA required
    Best for NatWest business customers with regular overseas card spend
    Watch out £30 per-cardholder fee stacks up for teams unless each cardholder hits £6k+ spend
    Not ideal if You don’t spend overseas, or you don’t bank with NatWest
    Eligibility Turnover under £2m. NatWest BCA required.
    No FX fee is a genuine differentiator if your business spends internationally
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against natwest.com, 20 March 2026
    22% APR · 15.9% purchase rate · £32 fee waived year 1
    Worth it if you already bank with HSBC

    HSBC Business Credit Card

    Competitive purchase rate, but the annual fee is not waivable after year one. Not worth switching banks for.
    View HSBC Card Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 22% variable
    Purchase Rate 15.9% p.a. variable
    Annual Fee £32 per card Waived in year 1
    Existing Account HSBC BCA required
    Best for Existing HSBC business customers who want a low purchase rate
    Watch out £32 fee is not waivable after year 1. FX fee is 2.99%.
    Not ideal if You don’t bank with HSBC
    Eligibility HSBC business current account required.
    Low purchase rate for HSBC customers — but the fee sticks after year one
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against business.hsbc.uk, 20 March 2026
    18.9% APR · No annual fee · Branch application only
    No-fee card at a competitive rate

    Metro Bank Business Credit Card

    No annual fee at all, and a competitive APR. The catch: you must apply in a Metro Bank branch.
    View Metro Bank Card Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Representative APR 18.9% variable
    Purchase Rate 18.9% p.a. variable
    Annual Fee £0 No fee, no waiver threshold
    Existing Account Metro Bank BCA required
    Best for Low-spend businesses near a Metro Bank branch that carry a balance
    Watch out In-branch application only. Metro Bank’s branch network is London-centric.
    Not ideal if You’re not near a Metro Bank branch, or you need to apply online
    Eligibility Metro Bank BCA required. Must apply in branch.
    The simplest cost structure, but the branch-only requirement is a real filter
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against metrobankonline.co.uk, 20 March 2026
    £0 yr 1, then £195/year · Membership Rewards points · Charge card (pay monthly)
    Best rewards programme for high-spend businesses

    American Express Business Gold Card

    The most flexible rewards programme in the UK business card market. Charge card structure means no revolving debt.
    View Amex Business Gold Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Annual Fee £0 first year, then £195/year
    Rewards 1 MR point per £1 (2x on Amex Travel)
    Card Type Charge card with Flexible Payment Option (from Jan 2026)
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for Businesses spending £5k+/month that can clear the balance monthly and want flexible rewards
    Watch out Annual fee is significant. Amex acceptance is not universal in the UK.
    Not ideal if You need to carry a balance, or your suppliers don’t accept Amex
    Eligibility Sole traders, partnerships, limited companies, LLPs. Check americanexpress.com/uk.
    The rewards are strong, but it’s a charge card — you pay the full balance every month
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against americanexpress.com/uk, 20 March 2026
    £250/year · 1.5 Avios per £1 (3 on BA) · Travel perks
    Best for earning Avios on business spend

    British Airways Accelerating Business Card

    Purpose-built for Avios earning. If your business travel is on BA, this card converts everyday spend into flights.
    View BA Accelerating Business Card Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Annual Fee £250
    Avios Earn Rate 1.5 Avios per £1 (2 On Business Points on BA)
    Bonus Avios 10,000 per £20k spend (up to 30k/year)
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for Businesses with frequent BA travel that want to earn Avios on all card spend
    Watch out Amex acceptance gaps in the UK. Annual fee needs enough spend to justify.
    Not ideal if You don’t fly BA, or your suppliers don’t accept Amex
    Eligibility Sole traders, partnerships, limited companies, LLPs. Check americanexpress.com/uk.
    The direct route to Avios — if BA is your airline and Amex is accepted by your suppliers
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against americanexpress.com/uk, 20 March 2026
    £650/year · Centurion lounge access · Charge card
    Premium charge card with travel perks

    American Express Business Platinum Card

    The premium tier of Amex business cards. Lounge access, travel insurance, and the highest reward earn rate — at a premium price.
    View Amex Business Platinum Details → Opens provider website in new tab
    Annual Fee £650/year (supplementary: £295)
    Rewards MR points + 10k bonus per £10k/month
    Card Type Charge card with Flexible Payment Option
    Existing Account No existing account required
    Best for High-spend businesses (£10k+/month) that travel frequently and value lounge access
    Watch out Highest annual fee of any UK business card. Only viable at scale.
    Not ideal if Your monthly spend is under £5k, or you rarely travel internationally
    Eligibility Sole traders, partnerships, limited companies, LLPs. Check americanexpress.com/uk.
    The perks are genuine, but the annual fee means this only works at high spend volumes
    Read our full editorial take ▼
    Rates verified against americanexpress.com/uk, 20 March 2026

    Also Worth Knowing: Charge Cards and Niche Options

    These cards don’t fit the main ranking because they’re either charge cards (no revolving credit), niche products, or regional variants. They’re listed separately for completeness.

    Side-by-Side Comparison

    Rates verified against provider pricing pages, 20 March 2026. All variable rates may change after publication.

    How We Ranked These Cards

    We did not use a single scoring system. A card with a low APR is not “better” than a card with high cashback — they serve different needs. Instead, we grouped cards by what they’re genuinely best at:

    • Open access cards come first because most readers can’t access the bank-locked options
    • Bank-locked cards are ranked by APR within their group, since carrying cost is the primary differentiator
    • Rewards and charge cards are listed separately because APR ranking doesn’t apply to them

    This is an editorial ranking, not a mathematically weighted score. We prioritised access, then cost, then features — because a card you can’t get is not a card you should be comparing.

    To give you a concrete example: a business on Tide with £4k monthly spend has exactly three realistic options. Barclaycard at 25.5%, Capital on Tap at whatever rate it offers you, or the Amex range if your suppliers accept it. The seven bank-locked cards on this page are invisible to that business. That access filter shaped the ranking more than any APR difference.

    Questions We Get Asked

    Can I get a business credit card as a sole trader?

    Yes, but your options are more limited than for limited companies. Barclaycard, Lloyds, and Metro Bank explicitly accept sole traders. Capital on Tap does not — it requires Companies House registration. For the others, sole trader eligibility is either confirmed or not stated on their public pages. We’ve noted this in each card’s entry and in the comparison table.

    See our dedicated page: Best business credit cards for sole traders.

    Do I need a business bank account to get a business credit card?

    For most traditional bank cards, yes. Five of the six bank-issued cards on this list require a business current account with that bank. The exceptions are Barclaycard (no account required), all fintech cards (Capital on Tap, Moss, Funding Circle), and all Amex cards.

    What credit limit can I get?

    Traditional bank cards typically offer £500–£25,000. Capital on Tap offers up to £250,000 for qualifying businesses. Amex charge cards don’t have a pre-set spending limit (though they do have a practical ceiling based on your spending pattern). The limit you’re offered depends on your business’s financial profile, trading history, and the provider’s assessment.

    Should I get a credit card or a charge card?

    If you need to spread payments over time, you need a credit card. If you can always clear the balance monthly and want rewards, a charge card avoids the temptation of revolving debt. We cover this in detail: Business credit cards vs charge cards.

    Methodology and Disclosure

    Sources: All rates, fees, and eligibility criteria were verified against each provider’s public pricing page on 20 March 2026. Secondary sources (Finder UK, Uswitch, Merchant Savvy) are noted where used to supplement provider data.

    Rate type: All rates listed are variable unless stated otherwise. Providers may change them at any time after publication.

    Affiliate disclosure: BusinessExpert may receive referral or affiliate fees from some of the card providers listed on this page. This does not affect our editorial rankings, which are based on verified rates and publicly available product data.

    Regulatory note: This page is editorial content, not regulated financial advice. Credit products are subject to status and provider approval. We recommend comparing offers directly with providers before applying.

    Read our full editorial policy
